title: Taylor series
short: A polynomial approximation of a function near a point, built from its derivatives.
full:
A Taylor series approximates a smooth function near a chosen point by a polynomial whose coefficients come from the function's derivatives there. Around x = 0 (a Maclaurin series) the first terms are f(0) + f′(0)·x + ½·f″(0)·x² + …

Keeping only the first few terms gives a simple approximation that is accurate close to the point — for instance sin(x) ≈ x − x³/6 for small x. The more terms, the wider the range where it stays accurate.

The CAS tab expands around x = 0 up to a chosen order.
